qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in workflow import from URL endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF in a workflow import-from-url endpoint, which is unrelated to the advisory's Merge Node SQL Query mode arbitrary file write vulnerability. The advisory targets the Merge node's SQL Query mode, while this finding identifies a missing URL validation in a different endpoint.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via workflow import-from-url endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF in the workflow import-from-url endpoint via GET /rest/workflows/from-url, which is unrelated to the advisory's Merge Node SQL Query mode arbitrary file write vulnerability. The advisory specifically identifies the Merge node's SQL Query mode as the vulnerable component, while this finding targets a different endpoint and vulnerability class.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the qualifying vulnerabilities match the advisory-described vulnerable code. The advisory describes arbitrary file write leading to RCE in the Merge node's SQL Query mode, but both qualifying findings identify SSRF vulnerabilities in workflow import endpoints. These are distinct vulnerability classes affecting different components with no shared root cause with the advisory's Merge Node vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-hv53-3329-vmrm describes arbitrary file write in Merge node's SQL Query mode leading to RCE
Finding 'ssrf-workflow-import' targets SSRF via GET /workflows/from-url (different endpoint and vulnerability class)
Finding 'ssrf-workflow-import-from-url' targets SSRF via GET /rest/workflows/from-url (different endpoint and vulnerability class)
No finding references Merge node, SQL Query mode, or file write operations
